discovery_context: Discovered while running the 2026-05-04 emulator rehearsal of Challenge Tests C9-C12 on Pixel_9a (API 36) then CZ_API34_Phone (API 34). Manual rehearsal of the Internet Archive flow (the forensic anchor of clause 6.G) showed that tapping Continue on the AuthType.NONE provider login screen does NOT navigate to the main app surface. Same flow tested would presumably affect every provider (RuTracker, RuTor, Kinozal, NNM-Club, ArchiveOrg, Gutenberg) because the navigation handler is shared.
classification: Sixth Law clause 6 (real-user bug + green tests) AND a Seventh Law clause 6 incident (the Bluff Discovery Protocol).

the_bluff_layer_1.what: ProviderLoginViewModelTest.no-auth provider Continue tap succeeds without anonymous toggle (added 2026-05-04 in commit 32706b8) passes at the JVM/Robolectric layer. It correctly observes LoginSideEffect.Success emission AND container.stateFlow.value.isLoading == false after the AuthType.NONE short-circuit fires.
the_bluff_layer_1.why_it_was_a_bluff: The Sixth Law clause 1 says the test must traverse the production code path the user's action triggers. The test traverses the VIEWMODEL code path correctly. It does NOT traverse the SCREEN's collectSideEffect handler, which on Success emits `back()`. The screen's `back()` callback at the root of the navigation graph has no parent to back to, so the screen stays put. The test assertion 'isLoading is false + Success emitted' is true in production — but the user-visible outcome (navigated to main app) is FALSE. The unit test, by ending at the side-effect emission boundary, missed the wiring failure on the other side of the boundary.
the_bluff_layer_1.lesson: A Compose Side-effect-emission test is at most a VM-CONTRACT (per feature/CLAUDE.md classification). It does NOT satisfy clause 4 (Integration Challenge Test) because it does not assert on the rendered outcome of the side effect. The same kind of bluff caused the original IA stuck-on-loading bug (commit 49714c0). We re-introduced the same bluff variant in the regression test we shipped to prevent it. The commit message for 32706b8 acknowledges this in its Bluff-Audit notes: 'the matching real-device Compose UI Challenge Test (per 6.G clause 5) is still owed'.

the_bluff_layer_2.what: Existing Challenge Tests C1-C8 (per app/src/androidTest/kotlin/lava/app/challenges/) declared in the constitution as 'NOW RUNNABLE on a connected device' (Step 6 SP-3a 2026-04-30). Empirical run of C1 (App Launch + Tracker Selection) on CZ_API34_Phone today fails immediately: 'could not find any node that satisfies: (Text contains "Menu")'. The app's onboarding screen, after commit 7a0317d (mandatory onboarding, 2026-05-04), shows 'Select Provider', NOT 'Menu'. C1 was never re-rehearsed against the post-onboarding UI; it has been a bluff since 7a0317d landed.
the_bluff_layer_2.why_it_was_a_bluff: Per the Forbidden Test Patterns clause 4, a test that builds the SUT but never reliably exercises it is a compilation test, not a behavior test. C1's compilation passed every pre-push gate (the gate compiles androidTest sources via :app:compileDebugAndroidTestKotlin). Actual run was never observed. The Sixth Law clause 5 / Seventh Law clause 3 operator real-device attestation was treated as a forward obligation rather than an enforced gate.
the_bluff_layer_2.implication_for_C2_through_C8: Likely all 8 are bluffs after the same fashion — they target UI text from the pre-onboarding-mandate UI which no longer exists. None can be running green today without actually seeing the new 'Select Provider' screen. The bluff hunt MUST extend to C2-C8 next phase.

tentative_changes_reverted: ArchiveOrgDescriptor.verified flipped true→false (rehearsal failed — Continue tap does not advance UI)
GutenbergDescriptor.verified flipped true→false (same root cause as ArchiveOrg — no provider-specific test possible until nav fixed)
KinozalDescriptor.verified flipped true→false (same root cause; also kinozal.tv may be geofenced from the test environment)
NnmclubDescriptor.verified flipped true→false (same root cause; also NNMCLUB_USERNAME/PASSWORD not in .env)

ProviderVerifiedContractTest restored to pre-flip state — verifiedIds = (RuTracker, RuTor); unverifiedIds = (Kinozal, Nnmclub, ArchiveOrg, Gutenberg). RuTracker/RuTor remain verified=true at the descriptor level despite the nav bug — they have C1-C8 real-stack coverage at the SDK layer, and the nav bug is a separate UI-layer issue tracked here. This is a deliberate decision; the alternative (revert RuTracker+RuTor to verified=false) would also be defensible and is left to the operator's call.

release_blocker_status.for_v1_2_1_tag: BLOCKED. Per clause 6.G clause 5 + Sixth Law clause 5 + Seventh Law clause 3, the next release tag requires a real-device-verification.md attestation produced from the gating multi-emulator matrix (clause 6.I) showing the user-visible flows complete successfully. As of this incident's filing, NO provider's user-visible flow completes successfully (Continue tap does not advance), so no attestation can truthfully be produced. scripts/tag.sh MUST refuse to tag commits in this state.
release_blocker_status.for_v1_2_0_tag_already_cut: Already shipped. The same nav bug presumably exists in v1.2.0 (the mandatory-onboarding commit 7a0317d landed before tag). Users on 1.2.0 are presumably affected. Recommend operator decide whether to ship a hotfix or wait for v1.2.2 with full nav-fix + Challenge-Test rehearsal.

remediation_plan: Phase 1 (immediate, before next tag): fix the post-login navigation. The login screen's `back()` callback at the root of the nav graph must navigate FORWARD to the main app's home screen on Success, not BACK to nothing. This is a multi-file change: addLogin's caller in MainActivity / NavGraph must wire `back = { navigate(homeRoute) { popUpTo(loginRoute) { inclusive = true } } }` or equivalent. Falsifiability rehearsal MUST verify on the same emulator that Continue → main app works.

Phase 2 (same release): re-rehearse C1-C8 against the post-fix UI. Update UI selectors that have rotted ('Menu' is not the right text anymore — verify the new top-bar label by uiautomator dump). For each test that no longer matches, classify as bluff per clause 6.J and rewrite OR remove. A test that cannot be made to pass against the real UI is, by clause 6.J, theatre — not protection.

Phase 3 (same release): write Challenge Tests C9-C12 against the fixed UI. The skeletons added in this session are committed but UNRUN; they MUST be operator-rehearsed before any verified=true flip. Falsifiability rehearsal mandatory per provider.

Phase 4 (same release): produce the multi-emulator matrix attestation per clause 6.I. Minimum API 28/30/34/latest, minimum phone+tablet form factors, cold-boot only. Output to .lava-ci-evidence/<tag>/real-device-verification.md.

Phase 5 (separate work item, blocking next-but-one tag): extract the emulator-matrix orchestration from Lava-side compose to Submodules/Containers/pkg/emulator/ + a cmd/emulator-matrix CLI per clause 6.I-debt. Lava's scripts/run-emulator-tests.sh becomes thin glue. Decoupled Reusable Architecture rule.
constitutional_followups_added_in_this_c: Constitution clauses 6.I (Multi-Emulator Container Matrix) and 6.J (Anti-Bluff Functional Reality Mandate) are added to root CLAUDE.md, root AGENTS.md, lava-api-go/CLAUDE.md and CONSTITUTION.md, and all 16 vasic-digital submodule CLAUDE.md files. They make explicit that a single passing emulator is NOT the gate (the matrix is) and that tests-passing-while-feature-broken is a release blocker by definition.

scripts/check-constitution.sh extended with a credential pattern scanner (clause 6.H clause 5 mechanical enforcement that was MISSING since the clause was added). Same scanner uncovered a separate incident — see 2026-05-04-bridge-credentials.json for the parallel finding (real RUTRACKER_USERNAME/PASSWORD shipped in C2 source via a placeholder-claiming `BuildConfigBridge` object literal).

ProviderVerifiedContractTest.kt extended with explicit assertions per descriptor — flipping a `verified` field without an operator-rehearsed Challenge Test now fails CI immediately. The test ITSELF is the verified-flag's gate.

feature/login/.../ProviderLoginViewModelTest.kt: regression test for the IA bug class added (no-auth provider Continue tap), with a falsifiability rehearsal stamp documenting the deliberate mutation that triggered the assertion message. Acknowledged as VM-CONTRACT (clause 1 at VM layer only); the matching CHALLENGE-classified Compose UI Challenge Test for the IA flow is owed in Phase 2 of the remediation plan above.
reverted_reason: An incident record is a forensic record; it cannot be 'reverted'. The CHANGES recorded in tentative_changes_reverted above ARE reverted in this same commit; the incident itself stays.
